name: learned-hook
install_doc: Our own learned baseline: a per-line salience scorer (logistic regression trained on this repo's trajectories, research/train_scorer.py) applied in a PostToolUse Bash hook (competitors/learned-hook/compact_hook.py). It drops low-salience lines from Bash stdout, conditioned on the task prompt (read from transcript_path) and the command. Pure stdlib at inference; no adoption needed (it is a hook). Validated end-to-end via THOL itself.
notes: Sanity probe of the RESEARCH.md direction: does ~32%-line compression at 0.95 recall convert to real end-to-end savings without extra re-fetch / lost success? Compresses Bash stdout only in v1. Absolute hook path is machine-specific (this repo's checkout).
